chapter_summary: John the Baptist prepares the way; Jesus is baptised and the Holy Spirit descends; Jesus is tempted; Jesus calls the first disciples; Jesus heals many and drives out evil spirits.
main_lesson: Jesus is the Son of God who came with power and authority — to save, heal, and call people to follow Him.
memory_verse: The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel. (Mark 1:15)
parent_guide: Mark's gospel is the most action-packed — 'immediately' appears 11 times in chapter 1 alone. Jesus bursts onto the scene with authority. Key moments: baptism (v9-11), temptation (v12-13), calling disciples (v16-20), healing (v21-45).
application_for_children: Jesus has power over sickness, evil, and everything scary. He calls us to follow Him, just like He called Peter and Andrew.

verses.kjv: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
verses.little_bible: This is the beginning of the most important story ever told — the good news about Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: This is the story of Jesus, God's own Son.
verses.meaning: Mark opens with a declaration: this is not just a story — it is the gospel, the good news that changes everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus Christ is the Son of God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for coming to earth. Thank You that You are God's own Son and that I can know You.
verses.discussion_question: What does 'good news' mean? What is the best piece of good news you have ever heard?
verses.family_discussion: Why is the news about Jesus called 'good news'? What makes it so good?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today one good thing about Jesus.
verses.action_challenge: Write 'Good News: Jesus is the Son of God!' on a card and put it on your mirror.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open scroll or ancient book with golden lettering, rays of light bursting from the page. Warm, epic storybook style.

verses.kjv: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
verses.little_bible: A long time before Jesus came, God's prophets wrote about a messenger who would come to get people ready for Jesus. God kept His promise.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God promised to send someone to get people ready for Jesus.
verses.meaning: God's plan for Jesus was written down hundreds of years before Jesus was born. God always keeps His promises.
verses.memory_phrase: God always keeps His promises.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your Word is true and that every promise You make comes true.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a promise someone kept for you? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: What promises has God made in the Bible that we can trust today?
verses.do_it_today: Look up one promise from God in the Bible and write it down.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, make a list of five promises God has made to you. Put it somewhere you can see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: An ancient scroll with glowing text, a road stretching through a desert landscape, a lone figure walking ahead. Warm golden light.

verses.kjv: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
verses.little_bible: God said a voice would cry out in the wilderness: 'Get ready! Make a straight path — the Lord is coming!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Someone was coming to shout: 'Get ready! Jesus is coming!'
verses.meaning: John the Baptist fulfilled this ancient prophecy — calling people to prepare their hearts for the arrival of Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Prepare the way for the Lord.
verses.prayer: God, prepare my heart to receive Jesus every day. Help me be ready.
verses.discussion_question: How do you get ready for something really important?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to 'prepare our hearts' for Jesus? What might we need to clear away?
verses.do_it_today: Spend two minutes being quiet before you pray today — preparing your heart.
verses.action_challenge: Think of one thing in your life that is making your path 'crooked' — and ask God to help make it straight.
verses.illustration_prompt: A lone figure standing in a wide sandy wilderness, arms raised, calling out — a winding road becoming straight in the distance. Warm dramatic light.

verses.kjv: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
verses.little_bible: John the Baptist was in the wilderness, calling people to turn away from their sins and be baptised — washed clean — so their sins could be forgiven.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John told people to say sorry for their sins and be washed in water.
verses.meaning: Baptism was a public sign of turning away from sin and toward God. Repentance means changing your mind and your direction.
verses.memory_phrase: Turn away from sin and be forgiven.
verses.prayer: God, help me turn away from the things I do wrong. Thank You that You forgive me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to say sorry and really mean it?
verses.family_discussion: What is repentance? How is it more than just saying sorry?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something you did wrong this week. Say sorry to God — and to the person if you need to.
verses.action_challenge: Draw a picture of a person at a river being washed clean — to remind you that God forgives our sins.
verses.illustration_prompt: A river in a wilderness landscape, a robed figure standing in the water, a crowd on the bank. Clear blue water and golden light.

verses.kjv: And there went out unto him all the land of Judaea, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.
verses.little_bible: People came from all over Judea and Jerusalem to hear John. They were baptised in the River Jordan, telling God about their sins.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Crowds of people came to the river to hear John and be baptised.
verses.meaning: The response to John's message was massive — the whole region was stirred. People were hungry for God.
verses.memory_phrase: Many people came to be baptised.
verses.prayer: God, stir my heart the way You stirred the hearts of people who went to John. I want to come to You too.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think so many people went to hear John?
verses.family_discussion: When people are hungry for God, they travel far and do hard things. What are we willing to do to draw close to God?
verses.do_it_today: Come to God today as if it is the very first time — with a fresh, open heart.
verses.action_challenge: Read or listen to a passage of the Bible together as a family — like the crowds going to hear John.
verses.illustration_prompt: A wide river with crowds of people wading in and standing on the banks. A central figure in white baptising them. Blue water, warm sky.

verses.kjv: And John was clothed with camel's hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;
verses.little_bible: John was not rich or fancy. He wore rough camel-hair clothes and a leather belt, and he ate locusts and wild honey out in the wilderness.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John wore rough clothes and ate wild food. He lived simply in the wilderness.
verses.meaning: John's rugged, simple life showed that his message, not his appearance, was what mattered. He was like the ancient prophet Elijah.
verses.memory_phrase: John lived simply and trusted God.
verses.prayer: God, help me care more about what I say and do for You than about how I look.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think John dressed and ate so simply? Does that tell us something about him?
verses.family_discussion: We live in a world that values looking good and having lots of things. What did John's life say about what truly matters?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to do something simple today — eat a simple meal, give something away.
verses.action_challenge: Talk as a family about one thing you own that you could give to someone who needs it more.
verses.illustration_prompt: A rugged man in camel-hair clothing standing in a desert landscape, eating honey from a wild honeycomb. Simple and powerful. Warm earthy tones.

verses.kjv: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
verses.little_bible: John preached: 'Someone is coming who is so much greater than me — I am not even worthy to kneel down and untie His sandals.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'Someone far greater than me is coming soon!'
verses.meaning: John points entirely away from himself to Jesus. True greatness is knowing who Jesus is and making much of Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is greater than anyone.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are greater than anything and anyone. I worship You.
verses.discussion_question: John said he was not even worthy to untie Jesus' shoes. What does that tell us about how great Jesus is?
verses.family_discussion: In a world full of famous people, why is Jesus greater than all of them?
verses.do_it_today: Think of the most impressive person you know. Now think: Jesus is infinitely greater.
verses.action_challenge: Make a list of five things that make Jesus great and read it aloud as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A humble figure gesturing toward a distant glowing light on the horizon. The speaker looks small beside the light he is pointing to. Warm reverent tones.

verses.kjv: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'I baptise you with water, but the One coming after me will baptise you with the Holy Spirit — something far greater.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John used water, but Jesus gives the Holy Spirit.
verses.meaning: Water baptism was a sign. The Holy Spirit is the real thing — God Himself living inside those who believe.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus gives the Holy Spirit.
verses.prayer: Holy Spirit, come and fill me. Help me to know God from the inside out.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between washing with water on the outside and the Holy Spirit changing you on the inside?
verses.family_discussion: The Holy Spirit is God living inside us. What difference should that make in how we live?
verses.do_it_today: Ask the Holy Spirit to help you in one specific way today.
verses.action_challenge: Learn a simple prayer to invite the Holy Spirit each morning — and say it together for seven days.
verses.illustration_prompt: A gentle dove descending in a beam of warm light, with water droplets transforming into radiant golden light. Peaceful, reverent atmosphere.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptised by John in the River Jordan.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came to the river and was baptised by John.
verses.meaning: Jesus — the sinless Son of God — was baptised not because He needed forgiveness but to identify fully with humanity and launch His mission.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came to the river and was baptised.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for humbling Yourself to be baptised — to show us the way and to be one of us.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus did not need to be baptised — He never sinned. Why do you think He did it anyway?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus identified with ordinary people by being baptised. How does it make you feel that He wanted to be like us?
verses.do_it_today: If you have been baptised, ask your parents to tell you the story. If not, talk about what it means.
verses.action_challenge: Find a picture or video of a baptism. Talk about what is happening and what it means.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing waist-deep in the Jordan River, John the Baptist beside Him, hands raised. Blue water, calm sky, morning light.

verses.kjv: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
verses.little_bible: As soon as Jesus came up out of the water, He saw the sky open up and the Holy Spirit come down on Him like a dove.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When Jesus came out of the water, the sky opened and a dove came down.
verses.meaning: The heavens opening and the Spirit descending shows that Jesus' baptism is a pivotal moment — heaven and earth connect. The Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are all present.
verses.memory_phrase: The Spirit came down on Jesus like a dove.
verses.prayer: God, You showed Your love for Jesus at His baptism. Thank You that You love me too.
verses.discussion_question: Can you imagine seeing the sky open up? What do you think it looked like?
verses.family_discussion: At Jesus' baptism, all three persons of God were present — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. What does that teach us about God?
verses.do_it_today: Look up at the sky today and think: God is real, and He showed Himself at Jesus' baptism.
verses.action_challenge: Draw what you imagine the sky looked like when it opened and the dove came down.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus emerging from the Jordan River, the sky parting with golden light, a gentle white dove descending in a beam of heaven-light. Awe-inspiring, peaceful.

verses.kjv: And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
verses.little_bible: A voice came from heaven and said: 'You are my beloved Son. I am so pleased with You.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God spoke from heaven: 'You are my Son. I love You.'
verses.meaning: God the Father publicly declares His love and approval of Jesus — before Jesus has done any miracles. Identity comes before performance.
verses.memory_phrase: God said: 'You are my beloved Son.'
verses.prayer: Father, thank You that You love Jesus. Thank You that because of Jesus, You call me Your child too.
verses.discussion_question: God said He was pleased with Jesus before Jesus started His work. What does that tell us about how God's love works?
verses.family_discussion: God's approval of Jesus was not based on what Jesus did, but on who He is. How does that help us understand how God sees us?
verses.do_it_today: Say this to yourself: 'I am loved by God.' Say it slowly and let it sink in.
verses.action_challenge: Write 'Loved by God' on a sticky note and put it on your mirror — to see it every morning.
verses.illustration_prompt: Golden light streaming down from an open sky, a voice represented as rippling golden waves. Jesus looking upward with a radiant, peaceful expression.

verses.kjv: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.
verses.little_bible: Right away, the Holy Spirit led Jesus out into the wilderness.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Holy Spirit took Jesus into the desert.
verses.meaning: The Spirit did not lead Jesus away from hard things — He led Him directly into them. Following God does not mean avoiding difficulty.
verses.memory_phrase: The Spirit led Jesus into the wilderness.
verses.prayer: God, when You lead me through hard things, help me trust that You are with me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the Holy Spirit led Jesus into the wilderness right after His baptism?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes God leads us into difficult situations. Has there been a time our family went through something hard — and God was with us?
verses.do_it_today: When something hard happens today, stop and say: 'God is leading me. I trust Him.'
verses.action_challenge: Write down one hard thing you are facing. Next to it, write: 'God is with me in this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A lone figure walking into a vast, empty desert landscape under a wide open sky. A faint glow behind — the Spirit's leading. Quietly dramatic.

verses.kjv: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan: and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus spent forty days in the wilderness being tempted by Satan. Wild animals were around Him — but angels looked after Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was tempted in the desert for forty days. But angels came and helped Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus faced real temptation and won. He knows what it is like to be tested — and He overcame. Angels ministered to Him, showing He was not abandoned.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus was tempted but angels helped Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You know what it feels like to be tempted. Help me when I am tempted. Send Your help.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think it means to be tempted? Is Jesus able to help us when we are tempted?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus faced temptation and won. How does knowing that help us when we face temptation?
verses.do_it_today: When you feel tempted to do something wrong, say: 'Jesus, help me. You overcame — I can too.'
verses.action_challenge: Talk about one temptation that is hard for your family. Pray together and ask God to help you win.
verses.illustration_prompt: A lone figure in a rocky desert landscape, with wild animals nearby and glowing angelic figures attending to Him. Contrast of danger and heavenly care.

verses.kjv: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
verses.little_bible: After John was put in prison, Jesus came to Galilee and began preaching the good news — the good news of God's kingdom.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to Galilee and began telling people the good news about God's kingdom.
verses.meaning: Even when John's voice was silenced by imprisonment, Jesus stepped forward. The gospel cannot be stopped.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came preaching the good news.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for bringing the good news. Help me share it with others.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'the kingdom of God' means?
verses.family_discussion: The good news of the kingdom is that God is in charge and He is good. How does that change how we see the world?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone one piece of good news from the Bible today.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, memorise Mark 1:15 together this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking into a bustling Galilean village, people turning to listen. Warm light, crowds gathering. Energy and anticipation in the scene.

verses.kjv: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The time has come! God's kingdom is here right now. Turn away from sin and believe the good news!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'God's kingdom is here! Believe the good news!'
verses.meaning: This is Jesus' first recorded sermon. Everything has been pointing to this moment. The response He calls for is repentance and faith.
verses.memory_phrase: Repent and believe the good news.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. Help me turn away from wrong and trust in Your good news every day.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'repent'? What does it mean to 'believe' the gospel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said the kingdom of God is 'at hand' — right here, right now. How does God's kingdom show up in our everyday life?
verses.do_it_today: Say this prayer: 'Jesus, I believe Your good news is true. I turn to You.'
verses.action_challenge: Memorise Mark 1:15 as a family and say it together at dinner for one week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a hillside, arms open, speaking to an amazed crowd. Golden dawn light behind Him. Powerful and welcoming.

verses.kjv: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
verses.little_bible: As Jesus walked along the Sea of Galilee, He saw two brothers — Simon and Andrew — throwing their fishing net into the water. They were fishermen.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus walked by the sea and saw two fishermen throwing their nets.
verses.meaning: Jesus chose ordinary working people for His first disciples. No one is too ordinary for Jesus to call.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus called ordinary people to follow Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I may feel ordinary, but I know You can call me and use me too.
verses.discussion_question: Simon and Andrew were just fishermen — ordinary workers. Why do you think Jesus chose them?
verses.family_discussion: God uses ordinary people to do extraordinary things. What ordinary things does our family do that God could use?
verses.do_it_today: Whatever ordinary thing you do today, do it as if Jesus is right beside you — because He is.
verses.action_challenge: Talk about one ordinary talent in your family that could be used to help or bless someone this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two fishermen in a small wooden boat on a shimmering sea, casting a net. Jesus walking along the shore toward them. Morning light on the water.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to them: 'Follow Me, and I will make you fishers of men — people who bring others to God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Follow Me! I will teach you to bring people to God.'
verses.meaning: Jesus transforms the very thing you are good at — fishing — into your calling. He meets people where they are and redirects their gifts.
verses.memory_phrase: Follow Jesus — He will make you fishers of men.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to follow You. Use what I am good at to bring others to You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be a 'fisher of men'?
verses.family_discussion: What gifts has God given our family? How could those gifts be used to bring others to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person who does not know Jesus. Pray for them today.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, pray for one friend or neighbour each day this week who needs to hear about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing toward the sea and then toward a distant village, with two fishermen standing up from their boat, listening intently. Morning sunlight.

verses.kjv: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
verses.little_bible: Immediately, Simon and Andrew left their nets and followed Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Right away, Simon and Andrew left their nets and went with Jesus.
verses.meaning: Their response was instant and complete. They left behind their livelihood to follow Jesus. Discipleship costs something.
verses.memory_phrase: They left everything and followed Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me follow You quickly and completely — not slowly or halfheartedly.
verses.discussion_question: The brothers left their nets immediately. What would be hardest for you to leave behind to follow Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Following Jesus is not passive — it is an active choice. What does following Jesus require from our family?
verses.do_it_today: Is there anything you are holding onto that is getting in the way of following Jesus? Tell God about it.
verses.action_challenge: Each family member: name one thing you want to offer to Jesus this week — your time, your talent, your plans.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two fishermen walking away from a boat and nets on the shore, following a figure ahead on the path. Purposeful, forward-moving energy. Morning light.

verses.kjv: And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
verses.little_bible: A little further along, Jesus saw two more brothers — James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in their boat, fixing their nets.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw two more brothers in a boat, fixing their nets.
verses.meaning: Jesus continued calling. Every person He passes is an opportunity. He saw James and John at their ordinary work.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus kept calling more people to follow Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to be ready when You call me — even when I am in the middle of ordinary things.
verses.discussion_question: James and John were just fixing nets. How did Jesus find them? Does He find us in ordinary moments too?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus met people in everyday life — working, fishing. Where do you encounter Jesus in your everyday life?
verses.do_it_today: Stay alert today for a moment when Jesus might be calling you to do something — big or small.
verses.action_challenge: Look for one unexpected moment this week where God might be calling your family to act.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two young men in a fishing boat, bent over nets in the golden morning light. Jesus on the shore, pausing and looking at them. Gentle, watchful.

verses.kjv: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus called James and John right away. They left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers — and they went with Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: James and John heard Jesus call and they went with Him straight away.
verses.meaning: Following Jesus meant leaving the security of family and work. It was a real cost — and they paid it without hesitation.
verses.memory_phrase: James and John left everything to follow Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, give me courage to follow You even when it costs something.
verses.discussion_question: James and John left their father and their business. That was a big sacrifice. Why do you think they did it?
verses.family_discussion: Following Jesus sometimes costs us something. What might it cost our family to truly follow Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Thank someone in your family who has made sacrifices for your faith — a parent, a grandparent.
verses.action_challenge: Talk as a family about what you have given up — or what you would be willing to give up — to follow Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two young men stepping out of a boat, waving to an older man who waves back. Jesus walking ahead on the shore. A mix of farewell and excitement.

verses.kjv: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.
verses.little_bible: Jesus and His disciples went to Capernaum. On the Sabbath day, Jesus went into the synagogue — the Jewish place of worship — and began to teach.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to the synagogue on the Sabbath and taught the people.
verses.meaning: Jesus went to the synagogue — He honoured the Sabbath. Then He taught, not like scribes who quoted others but with His own authority.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus went to worship and then He taught.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to come to worship with an open heart, ready to learn from You.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you go to church or a place of worship? How is it like what happened in the synagogue?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus made worship and teaching a priority. How do we prioritise worship as a family?
verses.do_it_today: Come to your next church service or family devotion with a question you want God to answer.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, write down one thing you learned in church or from the Bible this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing in an ancient synagogue, teaching to a crowd of seated listeners. Warm lamp light, stone walls. People leaning in attentively.

verses.kjv: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.
verses.little_bible: The people were amazed at Jesus' teaching. He taught like someone who truly had authority — not like the scribes who always quoted other people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The people were amazed — Jesus taught with real power and authority.
verses.meaning: The scribes quoted authorities; Jesus spoke as the authority. His teaching had a ring of absolute truth that stunned people.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus taught with real authority.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your words have power. Help me to listen to You above every other voice.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between someone who talks about what others say and someone who speaks with authority themselves?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is the ultimate authority on life, truth, and how to live. How do we let His words be our authority?
verses.do_it_today: Read one teaching of Jesus today and ask: 'What is He saying to me right now?'
verses.action_challenge: Find one teaching of Jesus in the Gospels that challenges your family. Read and discuss it together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Wide-eyed crowd listening to Jesus in a synagogue. People leaning forward, hands raised, expressions of amazement. Jesus speaks with calm confidence.

verses.kjv: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
verses.little_bible: There was a man in the synagogue who was controlled by an evil spirit. The evil spirit cried out.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A man with an evil spirit was in the synagogue. He cried out loudly.
verses.meaning: Evil cannot stay quiet in the presence of Jesus. The unclean spirit was immediately disturbed by Jesus' holy presence.
verses.memory_phrase: Evil cannot be silent near Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You are more powerful than anything evil or scary.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the evil spirit was so upset when Jesus was nearby?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' presence makes evil uncomfortable. How does His presence change the atmosphere in our home?
verses.do_it_today: Ask Jesus to fill your home with His presence today.
verses.action_challenge: Pray together as a family for protection — that God's presence would fill your home and push out anything that does not belong.
verses.illustration_prompt: A synagogue scene suddenly disrupted as a man shouts out, people turning in shock, Jesus standing calmly in the foreground. High contrast of chaos and calm.

verses.kjv: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
verses.little_bible: The evil spirit shouted: 'Leave us alone! What do You want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are — You are the Holy One of God!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The evil spirit shouted: 'I know who You are — You are the Holy One of God!'
verses.meaning: The demon recognised Jesus immediately — even demons know who He is. They called Him 'the Holy One of God' — an involuntary confession of truth.
verses.memory_phrase: Even evil spirits know Jesus is the Holy One of God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Holy One. I worship You. No evil can stand before You.
verses.discussion_question: Even the evil spirit knew who Jesus was. What does that tell you about how powerful Jesus is?
verses.family_discussion: The evil spirit told the truth about Jesus without wanting to. What truth about Jesus do we need to say more often?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'Jesus is the Holy One of God.' Say it like you mean it.
verses.action_challenge: Look up the phrase 'Holy One of God' and find other places it is used in the Bible.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man with a tormented expression crying out in a synagogue, spiritual tension in the air. Jesus stands unmoved, radiating quiet authority. Dramatic lighting.

verses.kjv: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus commanded firmly: 'Be quiet and come out of him!' — and the evil spirit had to obey.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said 'Come out!' and the evil spirit had to obey.
verses.meaning: Jesus does not negotiate with evil — He commands it. And it obeys. His authority is absolute.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus commands and evil obeys.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have authority over everything. Help me trust that nothing is too big for You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus just said a few words and the evil spirit had to leave. What does that tell us about His power?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus speaks with authority over everything — sickness, evil, storms. What do we need to bring to Jesus today and say: 'You have authority over this'?
verses.do_it_today: Name one thing that is worrying or troubling you. Say: 'Jesus has authority over this.'
verses.action_challenge: Write a short prayer together where you invite Jesus to exercise His authority over something your family is facing.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus pointing firmly, a burst of light emanating from His hand, the tormented man sinking as the spirit departs. Powerful, decisive moment.

verses.kjv: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.
verses.little_bible: The evil spirit threw the man into a convulsion and cried out loudly — then it came out of him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The evil spirit shook the man and cried out, then left him.
verses.meaning: Evil does not leave quietly — but it does leave. When Jesus commands, the outcome is certain even when the process is dramatic.
verses.memory_phrase: The evil spirit left when Jesus commanded.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when things feel dramatic and scary, help me remember that You are in control.
verses.discussion_question: The evil spirit made a scene when it left. Why do you think Jesus' healing can sometimes look dramatic?
verses.family_discussion: Have you ever seen God work in a dramatic way? Share a story of something God did that surprised you.
verses.do_it_today: Read this verse again slowly. Let it remind you: Jesus always wins.
verses.action_challenge: Talk about a time when God 'drove something out' of your life — a fear, a bad habit, a hard situation. Thank Him for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A burst of energy as a man collapses and then is helped up — the spiritual burden visibly lifted. Light flooding the space around him. Relief and awe.

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
verses.little_bible: Everyone was amazed and started asking each other: 'What is this? What kind of new teaching is this? He commands even evil spirits — and they obey Him!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone was amazed! They said: 'He even commands evil spirits and they obey!'
verses.meaning: The crowd recognised something unprecedented: Jesus has authority that no human teacher has. They were witnessing something totally new.
verses.memory_phrase: Even evil spirits obey Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You amaze me. You are like no one else who has ever lived.
verses.discussion_question: Why were the people so amazed? What made Jesus different from anyone they had seen before?
verses.family_discussion: When Jesus does something incredible, what is the right response? What should we do with our amazement?
verses.do_it_today: Let yourself be amazed by Jesus today. Find one thing about Him that blows your mind and share it.
verses.action_challenge: Research one miracle of Jesus as a family. Then ask: 'What does this tell us about who Jesus is?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A synagogue full of amazed faces — wide eyes, open mouths, people turning to each other. Jesus stands calmly at the front. Warm light.

verses.kjv: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
verses.little_bible: And right away, news about Jesus spread through all of Galilee and the surrounding area.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: News about Jesus spread everywhere very quickly.
verses.meaning: You cannot encounter Jesus and keep quiet about it. His fame spread not by advertising but by people who had witnessed something real.
verses.memory_phrase: News about Jesus spread everywhere.
verses.prayer: God, let news about Jesus spread through my school, my neighbourhood, and my world.
verses.discussion_question: The news about Jesus spread fast. Who first told you about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: How did your family first hear about Jesus? Who are the people in your story?
verses.do_it_today: Be part of spreading the news today — tell someone one thing Jesus has done.
verses.action_challenge: Each family member: think of one person who needs to hear about Jesus. Pray for that person by name every day this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A map of Galilee with glowing light spreading outward from a central point — like ripples on water. People in the distance turning toward the light.

verses.kjv: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
verses.little_bible: Right after the synagogue, Jesus went with Simon, Andrew, James, and John to Simon and Andrew's house.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went home with Simon and Andrew after the synagogue.
verses.meaning: Jesus moved from public ministry into a home — the private ordinary places matter to Him just as much.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came into their home.
verses.prayer: Jesus, please come into our home. Be present here with us.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus went to Simon's home. What do you think it felt like to have Jesus in your house?
verses.family_discussion: If Jesus walked through our front door right now, what would He see? What would He find in our home?
verses.do_it_today: Invite Jesus into your home right now with a simple prayer.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, decide one way to make your home a place where Jesus is clearly welcome — through prayer, worship, hospitality.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking through the door of a simple stone home, greeted by smiling disciples. Warm lamplight inside. Homey, welcoming atmosphere.

verses.kjv: But Simon's wife's mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.
verses.little_bible: Simon's mother-in-law was lying in bed with a high fever. As soon as they arrived, they told Jesus about her.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Simon's mother-in-law was very sick. They told Jesus about her.
verses.meaning: They brought their problem to Jesus immediately. That is the right instinct — when someone is sick or struggling, tell Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: When someone is sick, tell Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to bring every person I love who is sick or hurting to You right now.
verses.discussion_question: They told Jesus right away about the sick woman. Who do you tell first when something is wrong?
verses.family_discussion: When someone in our family is sick or sad, how do we involve Jesus in that situation?
verses.do_it_today: Name one person you know who is sick or suffering. Pray for them by name right now.
verses.action_challenge: Keep a family prayer list. Write down the names of people who need healing or help and pray for them regularly.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman lying on a simple bed, clearly unwell. Concerned family members nearby. Jesus entering the room, attentive and caring.

verses.kjv: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus went to her, took her by the hand, and helped her up. The fever left her immediately — and she began serving everyone.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus took her hand, helped her up, and she was healed right away!
verses.meaning: Jesus healed with personal touch — He took her hand. And her immediate response to healing was service. True healing produces gratitude in action.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus healed her — and she served.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when You help me and heal me, help me to use what You gave me to serve others.
verses.discussion_question: As soon as the woman was healed, she started serving. What does that tell us about how we should respond when God helps us?
verses.family_discussion: When God does something good for us, what is our family's response? Do we serve and give back?
verses.do_it_today: Do one act of service for someone in your home or neighbourhood today — as a thank-you to God.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, do one act of service for someone outside your home this week — as a response to all God has done for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus holding the hand of an elderly woman who is sitting up in bed, radiant with health. She is already reaching for a dish to serve. Warm, joyful light.

verses.kjv: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
verses.little_bible: That evening when the sun went down, people brought all the sick and those controlled by evil spirits to Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When evening came, people brought all the sick to Jesus.
verses.meaning: Word had spread about Jesus. People came from everywhere, bringing their most desperate needs. Jesus did not turn anyone away.
verses.memory_phrase: People brought their sick to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I bring to You right now everyone I know who is sick or struggling. Please help them.
verses.discussion_question: People brought their loved ones to Jesus. What are the 'sick' things in your life that you could bring to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like to bring our burdens to Jesus — not just physical sickness but worry, sadness, loneliness?
verses.do_it_today: Make a list of people or situations you want to bring to Jesus today. Pray through it.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, list three people or situations you are carrying a burden for. Pray for each one and commit to bringing them to Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Long lines of people coming to Jesus at sunset — some limping, some being carried on mats, some leading others by the hand. Warm evening light.

verses.kjv: And all the city was gathered together at the door.
verses.little_bible: The whole city gathered at the door of the house where Jesus was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The whole city came to where Jesus was.
verses.meaning: When Jesus is present, people are drawn. Entire communities are affected by His presence and work.
verses.memory_phrase: The whole city came to Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, draw our whole community to Jesus — every person, every family, every street.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the whole city came? What were they hoping for?
verses.family_discussion: If Jesus showed up in our neighbourhood today, what do you think would happen?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for your street, your neighbourhood, your city — that God would draw people to Jesus.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, pray a blessing over your neighbourhood. Walk around your street and pray for each house.
verses.illustration_prompt: A large crowd pressing around the door of a small stone house at night, torchlight flickering. Thousands of desperate, hopeful faces.

verses.kjv: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus healed many people with all kinds of diseases and drove out many evil spirits. He did not let the evil spirits speak because they knew who He was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus healed many sick people and drove out many evil spirits that evening.
verses.meaning: Jesus healed comprehensively — all kinds of sickness. He silenced the evil spirits because He would not accept their testimony about Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus healed many people that night.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the healer. Please bring healing to everyone I love who is sick.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus healed all kinds of sicknesses. Is there any sickness or problem too big for Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus healed physical sickness. But He also heals broken hearts, wounded relationships, and sad emotions. What does our family need Jesus to heal?
verses.do_it_today: Ask Jesus to heal one specific thing in your life today — and trust Him with it.
verses.action_challenge: Talk about healing as a family — physical, emotional, relational. Pray specifically for healing in each area.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus moving through a crowd of people, touching one person's eyes, another's leg, while others look on with awe. Golden healing light emanating from His hands.

verses.kjv: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
verses.little_bible: Very early in the morning, long before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a quiet, lonely place. There He prayed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus woke up very early and went to a quiet place to pray.
verses.meaning: Even after a full evening of ministry, Jesus rose early to pray. Prayer was the source of everything He did — not an afterthought.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus got up early to pray.
verses.prayer: Jesus, teach me to pray the way You prayed — early, alone, and with my whole heart.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus healed many people the night before. Why do you think He still needed to pray in the morning?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus, who is the Son of God, made prayer His first priority. How does that challenge our family's prayer life?
verses.do_it_today: Try praying in the morning before you do anything else — even for just two minutes.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, commit to one week of morning prayer together — even short — before the day begins.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus alone in a rocky, predawn landscape, kneeling in prayer, stars still faintly visible. A deep blue-to-gold horizon beginning to form. Peaceful, sacred.

verses.kjv: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
verses.little_bible: Simon and the other disciples went looking for Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Simon and the others went to find Jesus.
verses.meaning: When Jesus withdrew to pray, His disciples went looking for Him. He was so central to them that they could not proceed without Him.
verses.memory_phrase: The disciples sought Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me seek You the way the disciples did — urgently and with a full heart.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the disciples went looking for Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: When life is busy or something good is happening, do we remember to seek Jesus — or do we keep going without Him?
verses.do_it_today: At some point today, stop and actively seek Jesus. Ask: 'Where are You, Lord? What are You saying?'
verses.action_challenge: Practice seeking Jesus together as a family for one week — asking Him each morning: 'What do You want to say to us today?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Several disciples searching in the early morning among rocks and hills, one pointing toward a distant figure in prayer. Pre-dawn blue light.

verses.kjv: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.
verses.little_bible: When they found Him, they said: 'Everyone is looking for You!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples found Jesus and said: 'Everyone is looking for You!'
verses.meaning: The disciples were excited by the crowd's interest. But Jesus was not driven by popularity — He was driven by His Father's mission.
verses.memory_phrase: Everyone was looking for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to look for You too — not just because everyone else is, but because I truly need You.
verses.discussion_question: People were looking for Jesus. Why were they searching for Him?
verses.family_discussion: Are we looking for Jesus with the same urgency as these crowds? What would change in our family if we sought Jesus with everything we had?
verses.do_it_today: Search for Jesus in the Bible today — read one story about Him and let it refresh your heart.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, read one story about Jesus this week that none of you have thought about in a while.
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples excitedly pointing back toward a village as Jesus listens, head bowed in prayer. A moment of transition — prayer vs. crowd.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Let's go to the other towns so I can preach there too — that is why I came.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Let's go to more towns! That is why I came.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was not drawn to stay where the applause was — He was drawn to where the need was. He knew His mission and stayed focused.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knew why He came.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to know why You put me here and to stay focused on that purpose.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus could have stayed where He was popular. Why did He choose to keep moving to new places?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus had a mission and would not be distracted. What is our family's mission? What are we here to do?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God: 'What have You called me to do today?' Then do it.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, write a one-sentence mission statement for your home. What are you here to do together for God?
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing up and pointing toward distant hills and towns, disciples rising to follow. Purpose and direction. Forward movement.

verses.kjv: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.
verses.little_bible: Jesus travelled through all of Galilee, preaching in the synagogues and driving out evil spirits.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went all over Galilee, preaching and driving out evil spirits.
verses.meaning: Jesus' ministry was comprehensive — He went everywhere and left no town untouched. Teaching and deliverance went together.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus preached and set people free.
verses.prayer: God, let the message about Jesus go everywhere — to every town, every school, every home.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus went to many different places to preach. Where are the places in your life where the message of Jesus needs to go?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus went from place to place. How can we as a family bring the message of Jesus to the places we go?
verses.do_it_today: Go to one place today — school, work, a shop — and be a person who carries Jesus with you.
verses.action_challenge: Pray for one place in your community this week where you want to see Jesus at work — a school, a hospital, a neighbourhood.
verses.illustration_prompt: A montage-style image of Jesus in different villages across a green landscape — preaching, healing, people responding. Warm, expansive.

verses.kjv: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
verses.little_bible: A man with leprosy came to Jesus, knelt down in front of Him, and said: 'If You are willing, You can make me clean.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A man with a terrible skin disease knelt down and asked Jesus to make him clean.
verses.meaning: The leper had faith in Jesus' ability — 'You can.' His only uncertainty was Jesus' willingness. He was right about the ability; Jesus answered the willingness.
verses.memory_phrase: If You are willing, You can make me clean.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are able. Help me to trust that You are also willing to help me.
verses.discussion_question: The man said 'If You will.' He was sure Jesus could — but was not sure Jesus would. Do you believe Jesus wants to help you?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes we trust that God is able but doubt that He is willing. What does Jesus' response in the next verse tell us about God's heart?
verses.do_it_today: Come to Jesus today with one thing you believe He can do — and trust that He also wants to.
verses.action_challenge: Together as a family, come to Jesus with one big request. Say: 'We believe You can. We trust that You are willing.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A man with skin lesions kneeling in the dirt before Jesus, face turned upward with desperate faith. Jesus looking down with compassion. Humble, moving scene.

verses.kjv: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
verses.little_bible: Jesus was filled with compassion. He reached out and touched the man and said: 'I am willing. Be clean!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus touched him and said: 'I am willing! Be clean!' — and He meant it.
verses.meaning: Jesus was 'moved with compassion' — the deepest word for gut-level, overwhelming sympathy. He touched a man no one else would touch. He said 'I will.' He healed him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus said: 'I am willing. Be clean.'
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You are always willing to come close to me — even when I feel unclean or unworthy.
verses.discussion_question: No one else would touch a leper — it was forbidden. Why do you think Jesus touched him?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus touched the untouchable. Who are the people in our lives who feel like no one touches or notices them? How can we be like Jesus to them?
verses.do_it_today: Show compassion to someone today that others might overlook — with a word, a hug, or a kind act.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, identify one person or group of people who feel overlooked or left out. Do something intentional to show them they are seen.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling down and gently placing His hand on the man's shoulder. Golden healing light spreading from the point of touch. Compassion and power combined.

verses.kjv: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
verses.little_bible: As soon as Jesus spoke, the leprosy was gone. The man was completely clean.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus spoke and the man was instantly healed — completely clean!
verses.meaning: Healing came at the word of Jesus. Immediately, completely, irrevocably. He does not do partial healing.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus healed him completely.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You heal completely. Thank You that You do not do half-hearted work.
verses.discussion_question: The man was healed as soon as Jesus spoke. What does that tell us about how powerful Jesus' word is?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus healed completely — not partly. When God works, He does it fully. How does that encourage our faith?
verses.do_it_today: Thank Jesus for something He has done completely in your life — not halfway.
verses.action_challenge: Share testimonies as a family — times when Jesus did something completely — healed, restored, protected, provided.
verses.illustration_prompt: The healed man looking at his hands in amazement — skin completely restored. His face a portrait of wonder and joy. Jesus smiling quietly beside him.

verses.kjv: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
verses.little_bible: Jesus gave him a firm instruction and sent him away right away.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus gave the man clear instructions and sent him away.
verses.meaning: Jesus did not seek fame. He healed, then gave clear instructions about how to proceed — in obedience to God's law.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus healed and then gave instructions.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when You do something in my life, help me to listen to what You say next — and obey it.
verses.discussion_question: After healing the man, Jesus gave him instructions. Why is it important to obey Jesus after He helps us?
verses.family_discussion: When God answers our prayers, what is the right next step? Obedience? Gratitude? Both?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something God has told you to do that you have been slow to do? Do it today.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, ask: 'Is there any instruction from God that we have not fully obeyed yet?' Pray and commit to taking a step.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus leaning in, speaking quietly but firmly to the healed man, who listens carefully. The man is alert and ready to obey. Purposeful moment.

verses.kjv: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told him: 'Don't tell anyone yet. Go to the priest and offer what Moses commanded — as a proof that you have been healed.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Go to the priest and show you are healed — as God's law says.'
verses.meaning: Jesus told the man to follow the Law of Moses — to be officially declared clean by the priest. This shows Jesus respected and fulfilled the law, not bypassed it.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus told him to go and do what God's law said.
verses.prayer: God, help me to honour Your Word in all that I do — especially after You have done something good for me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus told the man to go to the priest before telling everyone about his healing?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus honoured God's law even while doing something miraculous. How do we honour God's Word in our own lives?
verses.do_it_today: Find one instruction from the Bible you can apply to your life today — and do it.
verses.action_challenge: Look up Leviticus 14:1-7 together — the law for cleansing a leper. Discuss why Jesus pointed back to this.
verses.illustration_prompt: The healed man walking purposefully toward a temple in the distance. Jesus watching him go, quietly satisfied. Sun behind, hopeful path ahead.

verses.kjv: But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.
verses.little_bible: But the man went out and told everyone. He spread the news so widely that Jesus could not go into any city without being surrounded by crowds — so He stayed in the open places. And people came to Him from everywhere.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man told everyone! So many people came to Jesus that He had to stay in quiet places.
verses.meaning: The man disobeyed Jesus' instruction — but his joy overflowed. His action shows how hard it is to stay silent about Jesus. Despite the disruption it caused, people kept coming to Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: People came to Jesus from everywhere.
verses.prayer: Jesus, even when things get complicated, people come to You. Let them come — and let me always be pointing toward You.
verses.discussion_question: The man could not stop talking about what Jesus did. If Jesus healed you, would you be able to stay quiet?
verses.family_discussion: The man's joy overflowed despite being told to stay quiet. What do you think is worth telling everyone about Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today what Jesus has done in your life — even one small thing.
verses.action_challenge: As a family, each share one thing Jesus has done for you. Then challenge yourselves to share it with someone outside the family this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: The healed man with arms wide open in a public square, telling a growing crowd. In the distance, Jesus stands in an open field as streams of people come to Him from all directions.
